decisions.rationale: Bench wall_clock fix (19.1-01) revealed prior under-reporting was measurement bug; canonical small zipfian clears 2s threshold by 1.27× margin
Locked in memory project_fraud_team_primary_bench; all Phase 19.2+ measurements should reference fraud-team K=10k zipfian as canonical complex-pipeline cell

decisions.decision: Same-key sketch batching FORBIDDEN as v0 commitment
decisions.rationale: Read-after-write semantic risk + Redis-shaped positioning per memory project_no_same_key_batching; Redis itself has no algorithmic same-key batching

decisions.decision: GeoSpread changed from max-distance-from-mean → RMS dispersion (Welford)
decisions.rationale: v0 not shipped publicly; current semantics counter-intuitive (mean-drift collapses outliers); RMS is what fraud teams actually want
Per memory project_no_sharded_apply; coordination overhead exceeds work for sub-µs apply; horizontal scaling via multi-instance Redis-cluster pattern

open_questions_for_19.2_discuss: Field pre-extraction storage: indexed array vs HashMap (lean indexed array)
HLL hasher: FxHasher vs cached AHasher (lean FxHasher)
EntityKey single-u64: Approach A (numeric only) vs C (hybrid, recommended)
Cluster storage: shared Vec<AggOp> vs split-by-agg_id (lean split-by-agg_id, simpler)
UniqueCells/GeoEntropy cap behavior: drop-new vs HLL-fallback vs LRU-evict
Cost-class doc surface: source attribute vs catalogue markdown

context_notes: Phase 19.1 family is fully complete with verdict PASS and ~26× lift on fraud-team realistic shape. Two opus research agents audited the 55-op catalogue: 67% can hit Tier 1 (~25-40 ns/call) post-Phase-19.2 wrapping fixes, 16% are at algorithmic floor (sketches), 17% borderline. Phase 19.2 consolidates ALL the optimization work — wrapping reduction (highest leverage), EntityKey clustering, op-specific tuning (UDDSketch BTreeMap→Vec, EventTypeMix allowlist), unbounded-state caps, cost-class observability — into one big phase. Stacked target: 13.4 µs → 7-10 µs/event = 100-150k EPS on fraud-team K=10k zipfian. The architectural anti-patterns (same-key batching, multi-thread apply, cross-event reordering) are locked in memory.
